title: lepidaria biflora
intro: Lepidaria biflora, also known as the Mexican feather grass or Mexican feather dalea, is a flowering plant native to Mexico and the southwestern United States. It belongs to the pea family and is characterized by its feathery foliage and pink to purple flowers. Traditionally, Lepidaria biflora has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. In herbal preparations, the plant can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, and its dried flowers and leaves are often used in potpourri blends and as a natural dye.
